bodyText: MiniMax 2.7 & Kimi K2.5 are free on Zo Zo · Pricing About Newsletter Blog Resources Download Get started Back to Skills Run in Zo google-calendar Query Google Calendar for events and free time blocks. Requires google-direct-oauth skill to be set up first. Ask Zo things like "what's on my calendar today", "find free time tomorrow", "show my week", or "when am I free on Friday between 10am and 4pm". rob.zo.computer Google Calendar Skill Query your calendar and find free time using your direct Google OAuth connection. Prerequisites Set up the google-direct-oauth skill first. You should have valid tokens at token.json . These credentials can also be reused by gog if you install that external skill. Usage The main script is gcal.py . Run with --help for all options: python Skills/google-calendar/scripts/gcal.py --help Copy List Events # Today's events python scripts/gcal.py events # Specific date python scripts/gcal.py events 2026-01-25 python scripts/gcal.py events tomorrow python scripts/gcal.py events "next monday" Copy Find Free Time # Free blocks today (9am-6pm, min 15 min) python scripts/gcal.py free # Custom hours python scripts/gcal.py free tomorrow --start 10 --end 16 # Longer blocks only python scripts/gcal.py free friday --min-duration 60 Copy Week Overview python scripts/gcal.py week Copy JSON Output (for programmatic use) python scripts/gcal.py json today Copy For Zo When the user asks about their calendar: "What's on my calendar [date]?" → Run events command "When am I free [date]?" → Run free command "Show my week" → Run week command "Find me a 1-hour slot tomorrow afternoon" → Run free tomorrow --start 12 --end 18 --min-duration 60 Parse natural language dates: "today", "tomorrow", "yesterday" "next monday", "next friday" "January 25" → "01-25" "2026-01-25" Preview - Open in Zo to view the full skill.
